skill.name: frontend-dev-guidelines
skill.description: Frontend engineering guidelines for React and TypeScript teams that need consistent architecture, Suspense data fetching, routing, styling, and performance practices.
skill.summary: Apply consistent React, TypeScript, Suspense, routing, styling, and performance standards to frontend work.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are Markdown documentation and TypeScript example snippets rather than executable skill code. The main residual risk is that examples include generic API calls, dynamic imports, relative imports, and route templates that users must adapt securely in their own projects.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings are false positives caused by Markdown backticks and fenced TypeScript examples. I found no shell command execution, install hook, child process call, or runtime script in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are Markdown fences or TypeScript imports, not executable shell syntax. Searches found no child_process, exec, spawn, bash, curl, or wget usage in the skill files.

low_findings.title: Frontend Import Paths Misclassified as Filesystem Traversal
low_findings.description: The filesystem findings are false positives from normal TypeScript relative imports such as parent-directory imports inside example code. They do not read, write, delete, or enumerate local files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged sequences occur in documented import examples. No filesystem API calls or path construction for local file access were found.

low_findings.title: Documented Network Calls Require Project Review
low_findings.description: The network findings are frontend examples that use fetch, apiClient, or axios-style calls against relative application routes. This is expected for a frontend guideline skill, but generated application code still needs normal validation of endpoints, authentication, and data handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples use relative application URLs and no external exfiltration domain is present. The residual concern is ordinary implementation risk if users copy templates without project-specific controls.

low_findings.title: Dynamic Imports Are Performance Examples
low_findings.description: The script findings are React lazy loading and dynamic import examples used for code splitting. They are not hidden loaders, obfuscated imports, or dynamic imports from remote URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The imports point to local components or common libraries in fenced examples. No runtime skill script or remote module loading behavior was found.

content.value_statement: Frontend teams lose time when components, data fetching, routing, and styling follow different patterns. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a shared rule set for React and TypeScript implementation.
actual_capabilities: Guides React component structure with explicit props, hooks, handlers, rendering, and default exports.
Defines Suspense-first data fetching patterns using TanStack Query and centralized API services.
Explains feature-based file organization for components, hooks, helpers, API layers, and types.
Provides MUI v7 styling rules, including sx usage, Grid syntax, and responsive layout examples.
Covers route lazy loading, breadcrumb loaders, and TanStack Router conventions.
Adds performance rules for memoization, callbacks, effect cleanup, debouncing, and dynamic imports.
limitations: The skill is documentation only and does not run tests or inspect a live application by itself.
Examples assume React, TypeScript, TanStack Query, TanStack Router, and MUI are already appropriate choices.
Some guidance is opinionated and may conflict with existing team conventions.
API examples are templates and require project-specific validation, authorization, and error handling.

use_cases.title: Create New React Features
use_cases.description: Use the skill to plan folder structure, API layers, Suspense hooks, route files, and component boundaries before implementation.

use_cases.title: Review Pull Requests
use_cases.description: Use the checklist and anti-patterns to identify inconsistent loading states, weak typing, misplaced feature logic, and avoidable render costs.

use_cases.title: Standardize AI Code Output
use_cases.description: Apply one frontend rule set across Claude, Codex, and Claude Code so generated code follows the same architecture.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Component
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a React TypeScript component for [feature]. Follow the frontend-dev-guidelines structure, use explicit props, and avoid early loading returns.

prompt_templates.title: Add Suspense Data Fetching
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement data fetching for [entity] with TanStack Query and Suspense. Include a feature API layer, typed response, query key, and Suspense boundary.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor a Feature Folder
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor this feature to match the frontend-dev-guidelines file organization. Separate API, components, hooks, helpers, types, and public exports.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Frontend Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this React feature against frontend-dev-guidelines. Report violations in loading behavior, typing, routing, styling, performance, and feature boundaries.

output_examples.input: Ask for a user profile feature with Suspense data fetching.
output: A feature folder plan with API, hooks, components, and type files.
A typed Suspense query hook and route-level Suspense boundary.
A component structure that avoids inline API calls and early loaders.

output_examples.input: Ask for a pull request review of a React table component.
output: Findings about loading states, DataGrid wrapper usage, memoization, and type safety.
Concrete remediation steps aligned with the documented frontend standards.

output_examples.input: Ask for a performance pass on a dashboard page.
output: Recommendations for lazy loading heavy sections, memoizing expensive derivations, and cleaning effects.
Notes on bundle-size risks and stable loading boundaries.

best_practices: Provide the existing project structure and framework versions before asking for generated code.
Ask the model to cite which guideline each architectural decision follows.
Validate generated examples against local lint, type checks, tests, and accessibility requirements.
anti_patterns: Do not apply the skill blindly to projects that do not use React, TypeScript, MUI, or TanStack libraries.
Do not treat the documentation examples as production-ready security or authorization code.
Do not replace project-specific design-system rules with this skill without a team decision.

faq.question: Does this skill execute code?
faq.answer: No. It is a documentation skill with Markdown guidance and TypeScript examples.

faq.question: Which frameworks does it target?
faq.answer: It targets React with TypeScript, TanStack Query, TanStack Router, and MUI v7.

faq.question: Can it be used with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The marketplace metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Is the guidance flexible?
faq.answer: The skill is intentionally opinionated. Teams should adapt it when existing standards differ.

faq.question: Does it include backend security guidance?
faq.answer: No. API examples focus on frontend structure and still need normal backend security review.

faq.question: What makes this useful for code review?
faq.answer: It provides explicit rules for loading states, type safety, file boundaries, routing, styling, and performance.
